title: Random but persistent outbound communications link failures w/ pasta
body:
### Issue Description

I replicate a MySQL DB using Apache Flink jobs in Podman containers. The Flink taskmanagers start up correctly, connect to the MySQL database (on a different server) and transfer multiple GBs of data. But then, after a **random period of time** (somewhere between 10mins and 2h), they **consistently fail with a 'Communications link failure' error.** 

I have spent a lot of time trying to debug this in Flink, Debezium and MySQL, w/o success. Then I tried the below netcat loop to test the connection to the database and realized something strange: 
- If I connect to the MySQL DB from the host OS that I run the Flink containers on, all connections are successful over multiple hours. 
- But if I run the netcat loop from within a Flink container (or even from within a Debian container) on the host, about 0.2% of the requests randomly return a fail. 

```sh
while true; do
	nc -vz $MYSQL_DB_IP 3306;
	sleep 0.1;
done
```

This brought me to the idea that it might be a Podman issue. Looking further, I changed the network backend **from pasta to slirp4netns**. After this, all the netcat connections from within the container are successful, and the **Flink job has been running w/o any issues** for over 10h. This suggests to me that there might be a bug in pasta but I lack the knowledge to dig further to be honest...

### Steps to reproduce the issue

The Flink job has a lot of Java and downstream dependencies. So here I just put the netcat example - since I strongly suspect they have the same root-cause.

1. Start generic MySQL DB on server A:

```sh
podman run \
  --detach \
  --name mysql-server \
  --env MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD='pw' \
  --publish $MYSQL_DB_IP:3306:3306 \
  docker.io/mysql:8.0
```

2. Open up port 3306 in firewall on server A

3. Start a container on server B (on a host w/ pasta network backend, e.g., Debian 13)

```sh
podman run \
  --detach \
  --name debian-server \
  docker.io/debian:12-slim \
  sleep infinity
```

4. Run netcat loop connecting to server A from container on server B

```sh
podman exec -it -u root debian-server /bin/bash
# Inside container:
apt update
apt install netcat-openbsd
while true; do
	nc -vz $MYSQL_DB_IP 3306;
	sleep 0.1;
done
```

### Describe the results you received

- Over a period of time (e.g., 1h), 0.2% of all netcat connection attempts fail. The failures aren't bunched together but appear individually and at random intervals.
- When running Flink, these connection failures eventually lead to the following error:

```
2025-09-24 11:48:30,978 ERROR org.apache.flink.connector.base.source.reader.fetcher.SplitFetcherManager [] - Received uncaught exception.
java.lang.RuntimeException: SplitFetcher thread 374 received unexpected exception while polling the records
	at org.apache.flink.connector.base.source.reader.fetcher.SplitFetcher.runOnce(SplitFetcher.java:168) ~[flink-connector-files-1.20.2.jar:1.20.2]
	at org.apache.flink.connector.base.source.reader.fetcher.SplitFetcher.run(SplitFetcher.java:117) [flink-connector-files-1.20.2.jar:1.20.2]

	... 6 more

```

### Describe the results you expected

All netcat connections to be successful and Flink to run w/o timeouts - which is the case after switching from pasta back to slirp4netns.

comments.body:
@usdn1 I let your loop run for several hours but I couldn't reproduce the issue. One notable difference compared to your setup is that I'm actually using two containers on the same host, I didn't have the chance yet to try something closer to your setup.

Regardless of the version, would it be possible for you to ask Podman to pass pasta some options, such as `podman run --net=pasta,--trace,--log-file,/tmp/pasta.log,--pcap,/tmp/pasta.pcap ...`, and share log and captures of a failed `nc -vz` attempt? Having both sides (client and server) would be nice, but the server side is the most important here.

Hi again - sorry for the delay, I was out for a while. The above Flink setup is now in production w/ slirp4netns so I would rather not touch it. However, I did retry the netcat loop w/ the `--net=pasta` option on the Flink server, as well as from a separate testing system where everything runs on pasta, and now I cannot replicate the issue anymore either... Both are on the old 0.0~git20250503.587980c-2.

Quite frustrating because the issue persisted for over a week until I changed to slirp4netns, and now it seems to have magically disappeared. Very confusing...

> However, I did retry the netcat loop w/ the `--net=pasta` option on the Flink server, as well as from a separate testing system where everything runs on pasta

Can it be that this separate testing system has different round-trip-times between nodes?

By the way, we recently fixed a couple of issues with handling/reordering of out-of-sequence segments (especially FIN). Maybe that was the cause of the original issue and you now moved to different implementations of virtual network drivers that avoid them somehow?
